info.title: Private Drop API
info.description: A self-hosted private long-text/file drop box API with Codex-like Project API for GPT Actions integration.

servers.description: Public server; runtime output uses DROP_PUBLIC_URL when set

mode.description: Context mode. Modes: overview=project summary+git status; tree=file tree; search=filename search; grep_context=in-file grep with context; read_file=read file with line numbers; markdown_outline=MD heading outline; read_section=read MD section by heading; agent_context=.codex/context.md; git_status=git status --short; git_diff=git diff; experiment_outputs=read-only aggregate of experiment output files, large files, gitignore status and git status. Designed for paper/experiment project audit.

path.description: File path (for read_file, grep_context, markdown_outline, read_section, or scoped tree mode)

query.description: Search query (for search or grep_context mode) or heading text (for read_section mode)

max_depth.description: Maximum directory depth for tree mode

content.description: Text content (for overview, read_file, grep_context, agent_context, git_status, git_diff)

path.description: File path for read_file, grep_context, markdown_outline, read_section, or scoped tree mode

query.description: Search query for search or grep_context mode, or heading text for read_section mode

max_total_chars.description: Best-effort total response character budget across all batch results

patch.description: Patch content. backend=builtin expects unified diff/git apply format; backend=codex expects Codex apply_patch format.

backend.description: Optional patch backend override. If omitted, private-drop uses the project's default_apply_patch_backend from projects.toml, falling back to builtin. builtin uses git apply. codex uses an external codex apply_patch executable after private-drop path validation.

diff.description: Best-effort git diff after patch execution, including failed codex backend runs that may have partially modified the worktree.

ReplaceTextEdit.description: Replace occurrences of old_text with new_text in an existing file. occurrence is 1-based; omit it when old_text appears exactly once.

path.description: Relative file path within the project

old_text.description: Exact text to find (must appear at least once)

occurrence.description: 1-based occurrence index; required when old_text matches more than once

ReplaceRangeEdit.description: Replace a 1-based inclusive line range [start_line, end_line] with new_text.

path.description: Relative file path within the project

new_text.description: Replacement text for the line range

AppendFileEdit.description: Append text to the end of an existing file.

path.description: Relative file path within the project

CreateFileEdit.description: Create a new file. Fails if the file already exists.

path.description: Relative file path within the project

WriteFileEdit.description: Write content to a file. Not recommended for small changes — prefer replace_text, replace_range, or create_file instead. Set allow_overwrite=true to overwrite existing files.

path.description: Relative file path within the project

allow_overwrite.description: Must be true to overwrite an existing file

CreateBinaryFileEdit.description: Create a new binary artifact from base64 content. Fails if the file already exists. Intended for PNG/JPG/PDF/WebP/GIF and other generated artifacts.

path.description: Relative file path within the project

base64_content.description: Base64-encoded file bytes. Decoded size max 5MB.

WriteBinaryFileEdit.description: Write a binary artifact from base64 content. Defaults to no overwrite unless allow_overwrite=true.

path.description: Relative file path within the project

base64_content.description: Base64-encoded file bytes. Decoded size max 5MB.

allow_overwrite.description: Must be true to overwrite an existing file

CreateBinaryArtifactEdit.description: Recommended high-level edit for saving generated images or binary artifacts from base64 content. Fails if the file already exists. Equivalent to create_binary_file with clearer artifact semantics.

path.description: Relative file path within the project

base64_content.description: Base64-encoded artifact bytes. Decoded size max 5MB.

WriteBinaryArtifactEdit.description: Recommended high-level edit for overwriting generated images or binary artifacts from base64 content. Defaults to no overwrite unless allow_overwrite=true.

path.description: Relative file path within the project

base64_content.description: Base64-encoded artifact bytes. Decoded size max 5MB.

allow_overwrite.description: Must be true to overwrite an existing file

CreateBinaryFileFromUploadEdit.description: Create a new binary artifact by importing a server-accessible upload/temp file. Fails if the file already exists.

path.description: Relative file path within the project

source_file.description: Server-accessible upload/temp file path. Allowed roots include project root, /tmp, /var/tmp, /mnt/data, and DROP_DATA/uploads. Max 5MB.

WriteBinaryFileFromUploadEdit.description: Write a binary artifact by importing a server-accessible upload/temp file. Defaults to no overwrite unless allow_overwrite=true.

path.description: Relative file path within the project

source_file.description: Server-accessible upload/temp file path. Allowed roots include project root, /tmp, /var/tmp, /mnt/data, and DROP_DATA/uploads. Max 5MB.

allow_overwrite.description: Must be true to overwrite an existing file

CreateBinaryFileFromUrlEdit.description: Create a new binary artifact by downloading from an http/https URL. Fails if the file already exists. Private/local addresses, redirects, credentials, and oversized responses are rejected.

path.description: Relative file path within the project

source_url.description: HTTP/HTTPS source URL. Download timeout 10s, max 5MB, no redirects, no private/local addresses.

WriteBinaryFileFromUrlEdit.description: Write a binary artifact by downloading from an http/https URL. Defaults to no overwrite unless allow_overwrite=true. Private/local addresses, redirects, credentials, and oversized responses are rejected.

path.description: Relative file path within the project

source_url.description: HTTP/HTTPS source URL. Download timeout 10s, max 5MB, no redirects, no private/local addresses.

allow_overwrite.description: Must be true to overwrite an existing file

dry_run.description: If true, returns a diff but does not write files

response_mode.description: Response detail. Use summary for larger edits to reduce timeout risk.

diff_truncated.description: True when diff output was truncated.

ArtifactRequest.description: Save a generated or imported binary artifact to a whitelisted project. Prefer op=save_generated for GPT workflows; it automatically chooses file_id, base64_content, chatgpt_estuary_url, source_url, or source_file in that priority order.

op.description: Artifact source mode. save_generated is the high-level GPT-friendly mode.

path.description: Relative destination path inside the project

allow_overwrite.description: If true, overwrite an existing artifact and optional companion markdown

base64_content.description: Base64-encoded artifact bytes; decoded size max 5MB. Used by save_base64 or save_generated.

file_id.description: Recommended upload reference. For save_upload/save_generated, resolves a /api/files upload id to DROP_DATA/uploads via the messages table.

source_file.description: Low-level fallback for server-accessible upload/temp file path; max 5MB.

source_url.description: External HTTP/HTTPS URL with SSRF protection; max 5MB.

chatgpt_estuary_url.description: Optional ChatGPT estuary content URL. Must match the narrow chatgpt.com/backend-api/estuary/content allowlist.

mime_type.description: Optional caller-provided MIME hint for audit/UI purposes

alt_text.description: Optional alt text used when returning markdown_snippet

companion_markdown_path.description: Optional relative path for a companion markdown file that references the artifact

companion_markdown_template.description: Optional template for companion markdown. Supports {{path}}, {{markdown_snippet}}, and {{alt_text}}.

selected_source.description: Actual artifact source selected by the server, such as file_id, base64_content, chatgpt_estuary_url, source_url, or source_file.

paths.description: Relative paths for diff/add/commit/commit_amend_no_edit. Required for add, commit, and commit_amend_no_edit.

command_text.description: Exact single-line command to request. Requires allow_raw_command_requests=true and is blocked if it contains high-risk tokens.

reason.description: Why this raw command is requested

request_id.description: Command request id to approve and execute

op.description: Command request or goal operation to perform

command.description: Configured command id for op=create or op=create_and_approve

command_text.description: Raw single-line command for op=create_raw or op=create_raw_and_approve; provide either command_text or script_path, not both

script_path.description: Project-relative script path for op=create_raw or op=create_raw_and_approve. The server validates the path and runs it via bash.

script_args.description: Arguments passed to script_path for op=create_raw or op=create_raw_and_approve

title.description: Goal title for op=create_goal or op=create_goal_and_approve

summary.description: Goal summary for op=create_goal or op=create_goal_and_approve

ttl_secs.description: Goal TTL in seconds for op=create_goal or op=create_goal_and_approve

request_id.description: Request id for op=approve or op=reject

request_ids.description: Request ids for op=approve_batch or op=reject_batch

status.description: Status filter for op=list or op=list_goals

JobOpRequest.description: Trusted active-goal scoped async shell jobs. create/create_batch require project and active goal_id; command text is audited to .codex/jobs/<job_id>.

op.description: Job operation: create=launch async job (requires active goal); create_batch=launch multiple jobs; check=run configured suite as async job; status=get job status+elapsed_secs (detail=basic: lightweight, no OOM; detail=logs: include log tails); log=get stdout/stderr tail with log_total_lines+next_cursor for incremental polling; stop=kill job; list=list jobs; summarize=Markdown summary with log tails; recover=metadata-only recovery after 504, no logs/process checks.

goal_id.description: Active goal id required for create/create_batch; optional filter for list/summarize

client_request_id.description: Caller-supplied idempotency key for create/create_batch/check. Reuse it after a timeout to recover the created job via create/list/status/log/stop/summarize.

suite.description: Configured project check suite for op=check, for example fmt, test, build, e2e, or full

command.description: Shell command for op=create; provide either command or script_path, not both

script_path.description: Project-relative script path for op=create. The server validates the path and runs it via bash.

script_args.description: Arguments passed to script_path for op=create

max_runtime_secs.description: Maximum runtime before timeout stop is attempted

since_line.description: For op=log: start reading stdout.log from this line number (1-based, inclusive). Omit or set to 0 to use tail-based reading (last tail_lines lines). Use next_cursor from the previous log response for incremental polling.

detail.description: For op=status: response detail level. basic (default, lightweight, no logs) or logs (include stdout/stderr tails). tail_lines only affects detail=logs or op=log.

elapsed_secs.description: Wall-clock seconds since the job started. Present for running and completed jobs. Null for pending or jobs where started_at is unknown.

oom_hint.description: OOM signal hint. Value 'possible_oom' if stderr tail contains known OOM patterns (Killed, CUDA out of memory, MemoryError, etc.). Null if no signal detected. Check this when a job fails unexpectedly.

log_total_lines.description: For op=log: total number of lines currently in stdout.log. Use this with since_line for incremental polling. Present only when op=log returns successfully.

next_cursor.description: For op=log: the since_line value to use in the next incremental log request to read only new output. Equal to log_total_lines + 1. Null if no lines were read.

metadata_only.description: For op=recover: always true. Indicates only metadata was read, no log files, no process checks, no OOM detection.

logs_included.description: For op=status: true when log tails are included (detail=logs), false when not (detail=basic). For op=recover: always false.

ssh_endpoints.description: Ordered SSH endpoints used for fallback. Empty for local projects.

default_apply_patch_backend.description: Effective project default patch backend when request.backend is omitted.

post.description: Aggregate endpoint for desktop tasks: create, list, get, claim_next, claim, or event.

get.description: List queued desktop/computer-use tasks for a future worker. Filter by status to let workers poll pending tasks.

post.description: Create a task that can later be claimed and executed by a desktop/computer-use worker.

get.description: Return one desktop task and its event timeline.

post.summary: Claim the next pending desktop task
post.description: Atomically claim the highest-priority pending desktop task for a worker. Returns success=true and task=null when no pending task exists.

409.description: Task is missing or not pending

post.summary: Update a desktop task with worker event state

post.description: Return configured projects and their runtime capabilities, including executor, checks, configured commands, and permission flags. Use this instead of relying on project enums in the OpenAPI schema.
Observe a whitelisted project: get overview, file tree, search, read files, git status/diff.

post.summary: Run multiple project context observations in one request
post.description: Batch up to 20 context observations for one whitelisted project. Useful for reducing GPT Action round trips; SSH projects also benefit from ControlMaster reuse.
Apply a patch to a whitelisted project. backend=builtin uses git apply; backend=codex uses an external Codex apply_patch after path safety validation.

403.description: Patch not allowed for this project

post.description: Apply structured file edits. Prefer response_mode=summary for larger edits. If this times out, do not retry immediately; first check git_status or read the target file to confirm whether the edit was applied.

403.description: Edit not allowed or unsafe path

post.summary: Save a generated or imported project artifact
post.description: Save a binary artifact to a whitelisted project from base64, a server-side upload/temp file, or an external HTTP/HTTPS URL. Internally reuses applyProjectEdit binary artifact safety checks.

403.description: Artifact save not allowed or unsafe path

post.description: Run fixed git operations on a whitelisted project. Supports status, diff, log, add, commit, and commit_amend_no_edit. Does not expose arbitrary shell.
Run a command configured in projects.toml under [projects.<name>.commands]. The request only chooses a command id; it does not accept arbitrary shell commands.
Create a pending request for a configured project command. The command is not executed until approved via command_approve. Requires allow_command_requests=true for the project.

post.summary: Run an aggregated command request operation
post.description: Single aggregated endpoint for command request workflow operations. Use op=list/create/create_raw/create_batch/approve/approve_batch/reject/reject_batch to reduce GPT Action count while preserving audit and approval semantics.

post.summary: Create an auditable raw command request
post.description: Create a pending request for an exact single-line command. Requires allow_raw_command_requests=true. The command is not executed until approved, is audited, expires like other command requests, and is rejected if it contains high-risk tokens.
List command request audit records, optionally filtered by project and status.
Create 1-20 pending command requests for one project in a single call. Commands are not executed until individually approved.

post.summary: Approve and execute a pending command request
post.description: Atomically claim a pending command request, execute the stored command_text snapshot, and record stdout/stderr/exit status in SQLite audit records.
Reject a pending command request and store the rejection reason in the audit record.

post.summary: Run or manage trusted active-goal async shell jobs
post.description: Active-goal scoped async shell jobs. Use client_request_id for idempotent retry. If create/status times out, do not rerun the job; first call recover with the same client_request_id. Use status detail=basic for cheap state checks; use detail=logs or log op only when logs are needed. basic is metadata/status-file based and may be stale for SSH jobs; use detail=logs, log, or summarize for deeper inspection. create/create_batch require an active goal. For op=log, use since_line for incremental polling. For op=status, default detail=basic returns lightweight status without OOM detection; detail=logs includes log tails.

403.description: Goal not active or project mismatch

post.description: Run a pre-configured short check command (fmt, quick test, build, short e2e) on a whitelisted project. Use only for checks expected to finish quickly. For long checks or anything that may exceed 30 seconds, use runJobOp with op='check' instead.
Write a GPT/Codex operation report to local storage and post a summary message to a channel.
